items.title: First ever talks to ditch fossil fuels as UN deadlock deepens
items.summary: Some 60 countries are meeting in Colombia as frustration grows with lack of progress in tackling climate change

items.title: Scientists discover a new way to prevent gum disease without killing good bacteria
items.summary: Scientists have uncovered a surprising way to influence the bacteria living in our mouths — not by killing them, but by interrupting how they “talk” to each other. items.title: Brain scans reveal a shocking difference between psychopaths and other people
items.summary: Scientists have uncovered a striking brain difference linked to psychopathy: people with psychopathic traits were found to have a striatum — a brain region tied to reward, motivation, and decision-making — that was about 10% larger on average than those without such traits. items.title: Antarctica is melting from below and scientists say it’s worse than expected
items.summary: Scientists have uncovered a hidden Antarctic threat that could accelerate global sea level rise far faster than expected. Deep beneath floating ice shelves, long channels carved into the ice appear to trap warmer ocean water, dramatically speeding up melting from below. Even regions of East Antarcti

items.title: Scientists reversed liver aging with young gut bacteria in stunning study
items.summary: Rebooting the gut microbiome with bacteria from youth may help stop aging-related liver damage and even prevent liver cancer, according to new research in mice. Older mice that received their own preserved youthful microbiome showed less inflammation, reduced DNA damage, and no signs of liver cancer
